req.title: Open savings account
req.statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a verified customer can open a savings account with an initial deposit >= $100, with tiered interest automatically assigned. Three primary test scenarios emerge: (1) EP positive — a valid deposit well above the minimum to confirm account creation and tier assignment; (2) NEG negative — a deposit below $100 to confirm the 422 error pathway; (3) BVA boundary — a deposit of exactly $100 (the lower boundary value) to confirm acceptance. An additional STC test covers the state transition from 'no account' to 'account active', and a DT test validates tier assignment logic across input combinations. All tests use a fixture that creates and cleans up a verified customer token, and a helper that posts to /accounts/savings.

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer via <verified_customer_token> fixture
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains 'tier' field with a non-null string value
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit(verified_customer_token, api_base_url, cleanup_account):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 500.00}

    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body, "Missing account_id in response"
    assert body.get("tier") not in (None, ""), "Expected a non-empty tier assignment"
    assert float(body.get("balance", 0)) == 500.00, "Balance mismatch"

    cleanup_account(body["account_id"], headers)

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_deposit
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer via <verified_customer_token> fixture
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing minimum balance requirement
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_deposit(verified_customer_token, api_base_url):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 50.00}

    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" not in body, "account_id must not be present on error"
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "minimum" in error_text or "balance" in error_text, "Error message should reference minimum balance"

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_exact_minimum_deposit
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer via <verified_customer_token> fixture
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a non-null 'tier' value
trace: R-FIN-002: minimum balance of $100 is inclusive lower boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_open_savings_account_exact_minimum_deposit(verified_customer_token, api_base_url, cleanup_account):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 100.00}

    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body, "Missing account_id at boundary deposit"
    assert float(body.get("balance", -1)) == 100.00, "Balance must equal boundary deposit of 100.00"
    assert body.get("tier") not in (None, ""), "Tier must be assigned at boundary deposit"

    cleanup_account(body["account_id"], headers)

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_state_transition_no_account_to_active
preconditions: customer has no existing savings account (state: no_account)
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer with no savings account via <fresh_verified_customer_token> fixture
GET /accounts/{account_id} to verify account status field
trace: R-FIN-002: state transition — customer moves from no_account to account_active
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_open_savings_account_state_transition_no_account_to_active(fresh_verified_customer_token, api_base_url, cleanup_account):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {fresh_verified_customer_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 250.00}

    post_resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {post_resp.status_code}: {post_resp.text}"
    account_id = post_resp.json()["account_id"]

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200 on GET, got {get_resp.status_code}"
    account = get_resp.json()
    assert account.get("status") == "active", f"Expected status 'active', got {account.get('status')}"
    assert account.get("tier") not in (None, ""), "Tier must be assigned after state transition"

    cleanup_account(account_id, headers)

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_tier_assignment_decision_table
preconditions: Three deposit amounts representing distinct tier partitions: 150.00, 5000.00, 25000.00
actions: POST /accounts/savings with the deposit amount and Authorization header
Assert each tier value is consistent with its expected tier label per business rules
Cleanup each created account via <cleanup_account> helper
expected: Each tier is a non-empty string distinct per deposit range
trace: R-FIN-002: tiered interest rates applied automatically
R-FIN-002: tier assigned based on initial deposit
